rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA BYRD VS. CYRONE BYRD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT CYRONE BYRD MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Hearing Required. Cynthia Byrd and Cyrone Byrd to be present in-person or via Zoom with video capability. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW KING VS. VAL-CHRIS INVESTMENTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And Supporting Memorandum Of Points, Authorities And Argument
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT GLENN AND CARRIE DONAHUE, TRUSTEES OF THE GLENN, JOHN LACY NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS AND SUPPORTING MEMORANDUM OF POINTS, AUTHORITIES AND ARGUMENT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to expunge lis pendens based on failure to establish a real property claim is DENIED. Plaintiff established probable validity of their claim for wrongful foreclosure based on the overstated amount demanded in the Notice of Default interfering with Plaintiff's ability to pay and reinstate the loan. Motion to set a bond is granted. Bond is set at $500,000. Parties' requests for fees are denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: RENEE POIRIER-POUND VS. CARROL TORRES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT GRACIELA. TORRES DEMURRER TO Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The veracity of allegations in the complaint cannot be challenged on a demurrer. The Court grants Defendant's request for judicial notice of the 2012 action against "Arturo Torres." The Court notes (1) that the Order Granting a Motion for Nonsuit does not make any reference to Mr. Torres's status as a tenant; (2) that the request for judicial notice does not include a copy of the motion for nonsuit and grounds upon which the motion was made; (3) that there is no legal authority presented for the proposition that the tenancy of Arturo Torres is the tenancy of the named defendants in this action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE 300 COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL PARTNERSHIP VS. DIGITIAL REVOLUTION MEDIA CENTER LLC A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 16, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF THE 300 COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL PARTNERSHIP APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is DENIED. A right to attach order will be issued "if the court finds at the hearing that the plaintiff's claim is probably valid." The only "claim" before the Court at this time is the verified complaint for unlawful detainer. As Plaintiff concedes that possession is no longer at issue, there is no probable validity to a claim for unlawful detainer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
